id = "algebra-rings/prime-subfield"

summary = "Every field contains a smallest subfield isomorphic to Q or to F_p."

+++

**Prime subfield**: Let $K$ be a field. The subfield of $K$ generated by $1$ is isomorphic to $\mathbb Q$ if $\operatorname{char}(K)=0$, and is isomorphic to $\mathbb F_p$ if $\operatorname{char}(K)=p>0$.

Apply [[algebra-rings/characteristic-zero-or-prime|the characteristic dichotomy for domains]] to the [[algebra-rings/integral-domain|integral domain]] underlying a [[algebra-rings/field|field]] to see that $\operatorname{char}(K)$ is $0$ or a prime $p$. The [[algebra-rings/characteristic|characteristic]] then controls the image of $\mathbb Z\to K$; in characteristic $0$ one takes the induced [[algebra-rings/fraction-field|fraction field]] to obtain a copy of $\mathbb Q$, while in characteristic $p$ the image is $\mathbb F_p$.
